timeline_html:
Summary. Leigh’s shedding has been present for over a year, coming in waves, with no clear baby-hair regrowth yet. Several overlapping long-term factors seem to have contributed.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Started or stopped birth control / hormonal treatment (ongoing; started Jan 2018)
  • Rapid weight loss / restrictive dieting (ongoing; started Jan 2019)
  • Chronic illness flare (ongoing; started Nov 2025)

Timeline forecast. The hormonal and weight-related drivers have been active for years, creating a slow, chronic shedding pattern. The recent autoimmune flare from Nov 2025 likely intensified loss, with the worst shedding expected around Jan–Feb 2026 and gradual settling by mid‑2026 if the flare improves. Baby hairs often start to appear 1–2 months after stabilization, though the ongoing hormonal and weight factors may delay this slightly. Dominant driver: Chronic illness flare (Nov 2025), as its timing aligns with the most recent worsening and it remains active.

Where you are now. You’re in the peak phase, when the shedding is most noticeable. As the flare calms and your system stabilizes, shedding should ease over the next few months—hang in there, better days are coming. 🌱
